evidence.command: uv run pytest tests/test_workflow.py -q -k "claim_auto_selects_first_idle_lane or claim_reuses_named_lane_worktree or claim_recreates_missing_lane_worktree or claim_retries_after_push_conflict or claim_creates_dedicated_worktree_when_required"
evidence.summary: Focused claim regression coverage for lane auto-selection, named lane reuse, missing lane reprovision, push-conflict retry, and legacy dedicated worktree behavior.
Full lane and workflow suites pass after the claim flow changes.

evidence.command: uv run pytest tests/test_workflow.py -q -k "claim_creates_dedicated_worktree_when_required or claim_rejects_active_lane_via_workspace_id or claim_errors_when_all_lanes_are_active or claim_rejects_dirty_lane_worktree or claim_retry_errors_when_work_item_status_changes or claim_retries_after_push_conflict or claim_auto_selects_first_idle_lane or claim_reuses_named_lane_worktree or claim_recreates_missing_lane_worktree"
evidence.summary: Addressed review feedback with coverage for active-lane rejection via workspace_id, all-lanes-active failure, dirty lane rejection, status-changed retry failure, and rechecked the legacy non-lane claim path.
Full lane and workflow suites remain green after the review fixes.

Blocked active-lane claims through both --lane and --workspace-id, made lane workspace preparation reject active branches instead of switching away from them, and added regression coverage for active, dirty, exhausted-lane, and status-change retry paths.
